treatment_information.treatment_response: Deterioration of paranoid symptoms and mobility, leading to discontinuation

treatment_timeline.event: Onset of mood changes, dysphoria, increasing indifference, and loss of impetus
Development of difficulties in walking and frequent falls
Referral to a neurological clinic; neurological examination revealed normal proprioceptive reflexes, positive Babinski’s sign, broad-based ataxic gait, and cognitive disturbances
Condition deteriorated drastically with visual and auditory hallucinations, paranoia, aggressive behavior, and inability to communicate
Drug therapy with neuroleptics initiated and later discontinued due to deterioration of symptoms
